title: Response Commitment, Problem Details, Streaming, and Downloads · Compiler Case: Download Range Filename
summary: Handlers write success before work completes, expose stack traces, send inconsistent problem objects, ignore stream backpressure, and assume errors can replace committed headers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts download range filename increment in single-owner response and bounded stream protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Serve downloads with authorized resource lookup, content disposition, safe filenames, media type, validators, ranges, HEAD parity, byte accounting, and abort cleanup.
Use stream pipeline, write backpressure, bounded buffers, cancellation, error ownership, trailers, and finish-versus-close evidence for large responses.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
